LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_UnderstatementCoda.label: understatement-coda
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_UnderstatementCoda.rule: End the entry with one flat understatement that shrinks what happened. No exaggeration, no punchline, no reaction.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_AbsurdDetail.label: absurd-detail
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_AbsurdDetail.rule: Include exactly one small absurd-but-true detail. State it plainly, then continue without commenting on it.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_PettyComplaint.label: petty-complaint
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_PettyComplaint.rule: Frame the event as a petty complaint about one object or the weather. Keep the facts accurate.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_MisplacedPriority.label: misplaced-priority
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_MisplacedPriority.rule: Mention one small inconvenience as if it mattered more than the event itself. Do not explain the mismatch.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_DeadpanInventory.label: deadpan-inventory
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_DeadpanInventory.rule: List what happened as a dry inventory of minor losses and gains. No emotion words.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_PoliteRegret.label: polite-regret
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_PoliteRegret.rule: Close with one over-formal, slightly too-polite regret about something trivial.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_SidewaysObservation.label: sideways-observation
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_SidewaysObservation.rule: Add one observation about an unrelated detail nearby, then stop. It should sit oddly beside the event.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_ConditionalCopOut.label: conditional-cop-out
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_ConditionalCopOut.rule: End on a flat conditional like it could go either way, with no real concern.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_RenamedThing.label: renamed-thing
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_RenamedThing.rule: Call one ordinary object by a slightly wrong dignified name, once, without explaining.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_ShrugCloser.label: shrug-closer
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_ShrugCloser.rule: Close with one short line that treats the whole event as barely worth noting.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_RoutineTerrible.label: routine-terrible
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_RoutineTerrible.rule: State the worst part as if it were routine inventory. No drama, no reflection, no pity.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_HouseholdMaintenance.label: household-maintenance
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_HouseholdMaintenance.rule: End with one quiet line that treats the terrible thing as ordinary household maintenance.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_UnacknowledgedDark.label: unacknowledged-dark
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_UnacknowledgedDark.rule: Let one detail be darker than the narrator acknowledges. They do not react to it at all.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_DryTallyOfLoss.label: dry-tally-of-loss
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_DryTallyOfLoss.rule: Tally who or what was lost in a flat, clerical tone, like checking off chores.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_GrimUnderstatement.label: grim-understatement
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_GrimUnderstatement.rule: Reduce the scale of the disaster with one calm understatement. Sincerity, not sarcasm.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_MatterOfFactMortality.rule: Mention death or injury the way one mentions the weather. Move straight on.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_QueuedForLater.label: queued-for-later
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_QueuedForLater.rule: Treat the terrible event as an item deferred to a more convenient day.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_ProfessionalDetachment.rul: Describe the aftermath with the detached tone of a routine work report.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_GrimPracticality.label: grim-practicality
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_GrimPracticality.rule: Pivot immediately to one practical next task, as if the event were already settled.
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_EvenMeasure.label: even-measure
LanguageData.DiaryHumorCue_EvenMeasure.rule: Weigh the day's loss against some trivial gain and call it even, flatly.
